title: Simple Models, Rich Representations: Visual Decoding from Primate Intracortical Neural Signals
research_focus: Visual decoding from primate intracortical neural signals

dataset.name: THINGS Ventral-Stream Spiking Dataset (TVSD)
dataset.description: Natural images paired with multi-unit activity recordings

research_questions: What role does temporal structure play in neural decoding?
What types of models are best suited to capture neural dynamics?
How do simple linear models compare to recurrent neural networks for decoding?
How do architectural choices affect decoding performance?
evaluation_method: Accuracy of image representation decoding using semantic representations from a frozen CLIP model
key_findings: Modeling temporal dynamics is crucial for high-level decoding tasks
Nonlinear models improve performance primarily through better temporal aggregation
Benefits of complex models are largely attributable to temporal structure rather than architectural complexity
